violations.text: Per interview of staff, slicers are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized every 6 hours. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. Slicer was sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

Deli meat in the walk in cooler was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

The concentration of the quat sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200-300 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025
An excess of ice build up was observed on the floor in the walk in freezer. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
Cardboard and litter was observed surrounding the outside cardboard receptacle. Refuse areas must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 06/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the of the gaskets on the prep unit draws, and low boy refrigeration units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non food contact surfaces must remain clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024
